frames: The agent starts in a low crawling posture, with its front right and rear left limbs making contact with the ground. Its body is slightly elevated, and the front left limb is lifted.
It shifts its weight, lifting the front right limb while the front left limb moves towards the ground. The body maintains a low, somewhat unstable elevation.
The agent's torso lowers significantly, almost touching the ground, as the front left limb makes contact. The rear right limb is also on the ground, supporting the collapse.
The body is now nearly flat on the ground, with the front limbs extended and bent, attempting to push forward. The motion appears chaotic and uncontrolled.
It begins to push its torso up from the ground, extending its front left limb forward. The rear limbs remain mostly flat, providing little propulsion.
The agent collapses its torso back down to the ground, with the front right limb extending forward. This indicates a repetitive, inefficient movement pattern.
It attempts another push-up, lifting its body slightly while extending the front left limb. The rear limbs are still mostly dragging.
The agent's body again falls flat onto the ground, with its limbs splayed out. It is struggling to achieve stable locomotion, exhibiting abnormal behavior.
